perturbation.original_text: Each Party will protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information by using the same degree of care (but not less than a reasonable degree of care) that it uses to protect the confidentiality of its own proprietary and confidential information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party will endeavor to protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This changes the obligation from 'will protect...by using the same degree of care (but not less than a reasonable degree of care)' to 'will endeavor to protect'. This lowers the standard of care, and creates an in-text contradiction regarding the measures required for protection of confidential information. The original text defines a specific standard of care, while the modified version introduces a vague aspiration, this lowers the level of care of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge and agree that irreparable harm may occur if any of the Confidential Information were to be disclosed to third parties or if any use were to be made of the Confidential Information other than that specified in this Agreement, and the Parties further agree that each shall have the right to seek and obtain injunctive relief upon any violati on or threatened violation of the terms of this Agreement, in addition to all other rights and remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text established a specific standard of care for protecting confidential information: "the same degree of care (but not less than a reasonable degree of care)". The changed text lowers this standard to "will endeavor to protect", creating ambiguity and weakening the obligation. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which emphasizes irreparable harm and the right to seek injunctive relief for 'any violation or threatened violation' of the agreement's terms. The lowered standard of care in the changed text makes it unclear what constitutes a 'violation' and creates a contradiction, making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information disclosed hereunder shall at all times remain the property of the Disclosing Party and no Party acquires any intellectual property rights under this Agreement (including but not limited to patent, copyright and trademark rights).
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information disclosed hereunder shall at all times remain the property of the Disclosing Party, unless explicit written consent is provided by the Disclosing Party, and no Party acquires any intellectual property rights under this Agreement (including but not limited to patent, copyright and trademark rights).
perturbation.explanation: This modifies the ownership clause by adding 'unless explicit written consent is provided by the Disclosing Party'. This introduces an in-text contradiction. The original clause clearly states that Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party at all times, while the modified version allows for a transfer of ownership if consent is provided. The original text clearly states that Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party at all times, while the modified version allows for a transfer of ownership if consent is provided, so it contradicts with 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information of either Party may not be copied or reproduced by the other Party without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states that Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party 'at all times'. The modified version allows for a transfer of ownership if consent is provided. The contradicted text states that 'Confidential Information of either Party may not be copied or reproduced by the other Party without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent'. The modified clause now contradicts the contradicted text because it allows for the potential transfer of ownership of the confidential information, whereas the contradicted text assumes the Disclosing party always owns the confidential information, creating a strong in-text contradiction under the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' type.

perturbation.original_text: Upon receipt of written notice requesting return of the Confidential Information, the Receiving Party will promptly deliver to the Disclosing Party (or, at the Disclosing Party’s option, destroy) all Confident ial Information furnished by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party, together with copies thereof. At the request of the Disclosing Party, any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing by Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon receipt of written notice requesting return of some Confidential Information, the Receiving Party will endeavor to promptly deliver to the Disclosing Party (or, at the Disclosing Party’s option, destroy) said requested Confidential Information furnished by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party, together with copies thereof, unless those copies are needed to continue a potential buisness deal. At the request of the Disclosing Party, any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing by Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: This modification changes 'all Confidential Information' to 'said requested Confidential Information', and introduces an exception: 'unless those copies are needed to continue a potential business deal'. This creates an in-text contradiction because it implies the Receiving Party can selectively choose what to return and keep copies under certain circumstances, while the original clause requires the return or destruction of *all* confidential information upon request. It contradicts the purpose of the agreement's termination clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall continue for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement, or until the Parties enter into a formal agreem ent for the provision of software and/or services by Snap Surveys NH, Inc., whichever occurs sooner. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall survive with respect to all Confidential Information that is disclosed before the termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the original requirement to return 'all Confidential Information' to returning only 'said requested Confidential Information', and introduces an exception for copies needed for a 'potential business deal'. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that the agreement 'shall survive with respect to all Confidential Information that is disclosed before the termination'. The changed text allows for the retention of some confidential information, while the contradicted text mandates that the agreement continues to apply to *all* confidential information disclosed before termination, irrespective of whether it's returned or not.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall continue for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement, or until the Parties enter into a formal agreem ent for the provision of software and/or services by Snap Surveys NH, Inc., whichever occurs sooner. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall survive with respect to all Confidential Information that is disclosed before the termination.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall continue for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement, or until the Parties enter into a formal agreem ent for the provision of software and/or services by Snap Surveys NH, Inc., whichever occurs sooner. This agreement will be considered perpetual, for only the Receiving Party, unless explicit written notice is provided. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall survive with respect to all Confidential Information that is disclosed before the termination.
perturbation.explanation: This change makes the agreement perpetual for the Receiving Party unless explicit written notice is provided. This contradicts the original clause which states the agreement lasts for one year or until a formal agreement is reached, whichever is sooner. Thus creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall continue for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement, or until the Parties enter into a formal agreem ent for the provision of software and/or services by Snap Surveys NH, Inc., whichever occurs sooner. This agreement will be considered perpetual, for only the Receiving Party, unless explicit written notice is provided. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall survive with respect to all Confidential Information that is disclosed before the termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a perpetual clause for the Receiving Party unless written notice is provided, which directly contradicts the original clause stating the agreement lasts for one year or until a formal agreement is reached, whichever is sooner. This makes the contract ambiguous regarding the duration of the agreement, creating a clear in-text contradiction as per the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition. Both clauses cannot be reasonably interpreted as compatible, since one specifies termination and other specifies perpetuation.
